examples: Auxin, for instance, stimulates the rate of cell elongation, causes shoots to grow up and roots to grow down, and inhibits the growth of lateral shoots.
This is followed, over time, by a permanent elongation of the eye.
You must be auxin, cause you are causing me to have rapid stem elongation.
It is often caused by an elongation of the eyeball that happens when people are young.
Exposure to light increases the levels of dopamine in the eye and this seems to prevent elongation of the eyeball.
